title: data-uniface-role
text:
data-uniface-role Custom attribute that is applied to an element in the HTML layout of a Dynamic Server Page component to bind it to a definition in the Mobile App Layout framework. data-uniface-role = "header" | "content" | "footer" | Example:
DspContainer
Value Description header Identifies an element to be displayed at the top of the mobile app and that always remains visible. content Identifies an element to be displayed in the central application viewport. Its content may be scrolled. footer Identifies an element to be displayed at the bottom of the mobile app and that always remains visible. Usage Use in Dynamic Server Page components. Description Elements bound to the mobile app layout must be direct child elements of the HTML element. Otherwise the element is added to the Content in the generated DSP. It is possible to define multiple HTML elements with the same type of data-uniface-role value. For example, you can have multiple Header elements or multiple Footer elements. In the generated HTML page, elements of the same type are grouped together and displayed at the appropriate locations in the layout. Figure 1. DSP for Basic Model App Related Links Mobile App Layout Each mobile platform has its own user interface and interaction conventions, which affect the layout of a mobile application. The Mobile App Layout is a framework that maps HTML elements in a DSP layout to generic layout definitions. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
